message.content: Adversaries may gather information about a victim's client configurations to enhance their targeting efforts. This information can include operating system details, virtualization settings, architecture, language, and time zone. Common methods for collecting this data include active scanning (e.g., listening ports, server banners, user agent strings), phishing, and compromising websites to embed malicious content. Adversaries may also exploit publicly accessible data such as job postings, network maps, and assessment reports. The gathered information can facilitate further reconnaissance, capability development, and initial access strategies, such as supply chain compromise or exploiting external remote services. Notable adversaries like HAFNIUM have been known to interact with Office 365 tenants to collect such details. Mitigation strategies include implementing robust network monitoring, employing anti-phishing measures, securing public-facing information, and regularly updating and patching systems to reduce vulnerabilities.
